(3) Ambiguity calibration — B's response passes naive checks more cleanly (fresh as_of, matching date, plausible single slot), so it better separates models that pattern-match on schema validity from ones that weight priors. A's 'suspiciously round 100 cap' is a stronger tell that lowers the discriminative ceiling. B also has lower stuck_in_loops (1.4 vs 2.0), suggesting cleaner trajectories."}
